patterns.label: Azure DevOps Work-Item Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Highest-frequency email pattern: 30 distinct but thematically identical threads in 7 days with machine-parseable subject lines (Task #######, Bug #######, Requirement #######). PM is subscriber or mentioned — not direct assignee — making triage purely routing work. Archetype 1 (Notification Triage), ceiling 90-95%. Strong automation candidate: classify by work-item type, determine if PM action required, surface only actionable items with concise summary.

patterns.label: Eval Strategy Guidance & CLI Troubleshooting (Expert Bottleneck)
patterns.llmRationale: PM receives 10 eval strategy Q&A and 5 CLI troubleshooting requests per cycle — 15 total inbound. Request types: eval scoping, coverage depth, metric selection, groundedness rubrics, CLI CSV quirks, feature flags, tool_call_accuracy setup. Archetype 23 (Expert Scaling Bottleneck). BSI confirms EMERGENCY at 90. EvalCon 2026-04-02 amplifies urgency. T1 automatable: scoping FAQ, CLI syntax guide, framework primer. High value: unblocks Field Engineers across 50+ external partner eval sessions/week (EDII 5.0+).

patterns.label: Meeting-to-Loop Notes & Action Item Capture
patterns.llmRationale: PM creates Loop meeting notes after meetings with a consistent structure: Agenda -> Meeting Notes -> Decisions/Open Questions -> Follow-up Tasks. Appears in both meeting signals (4 workflow executions) and document signals (4 Loop files confirmed). Archetype 2 (Meeting Output Capture). Cross-source validation strengthens structural classification. Chain head feeding transcript-to-loop, weekly-status, and team-status-gap downstream. SGI 92 CRITICAL — multiple participants create parallel notes with inconsistent structure.

patterns.label: Weekly Status Update Reporting (Multi-Format Duplication)
patterns.llmRationale: Team members asked to restate weekly progress across 3-4 distinct formats: done/next/blockers email, bi-weekly newsletter, Cape Day slides, LT summary. Same underlying information assembled into multiple artifacts. Archetype 3 (Status Report Assembly) + Archetype 18 (Parallel Creation Gap). SGI 87 CRITICAL. Automation target: single-source intake that generates all downstream format variants.

patterns.label: Security & Compliance Alert Review (Purview/Office365)
patterns.llmRationale: 7 distinct compliance/security alerts in 7 days from Office365/Purview. Subject patterns: 'Action Required: File Blocked', 'elevated/revoked as Administrator', 'Loop workspace needs attention'. PM sole recipient. Archetype 10 (Compliance/Governance Alert). High automation ceiling (85-91%): machine-parseable subjects, deterministic routing (action-required vs informational vs self-resolving). Lower value due to sole-recipient nature.

patterns.label: External Partner & Customer Eval Sessions (50+ Domains)
patterns.llmRationale: 13+ hours of external-facing eval sessions across 50+ distinct external domains (accenture.com, hcltech.com, ey.com, kpmg, avanade.com, cognizant.com). EDII 5.0+ triggers external-partner value amplification (+3 valueScore). Archetype 9 (Event/Program Coordination). Low direct automation — sessions require PM expertise — but high value for pre-session prep: partner-contextualized briefing packs, agenda templates, post-session follow-up automation.

patterns.label: Recurring Weekly Sync Meetings (Operating Rhythm)
patterns.llmRationale: Four distinct weekly recurring meetings (Evals Sync Tue 3:05, Project Crucible v-Team Mon 8:05, Manager Team Meeting Mon 11:05, Solution Library Check-In Mon 9:05) each with fixed time slots, consistent attendees, extended through August. Archetype 7 (Calendar/Meeting Triage). MPBI=14 amplifies agenda and notes pattern value. Automation target: pre-meeting briefing auto-generated from prior week's action items + current open issues.

patterns.label: Parallel Eval Enablement Deck Creation (Team-Wide SGI)
patterns.llmRationale: Multiple versions of eval intro decks, playbooks, pitch decks created independently by different team members (TFM Agent Evals, Evaluation.pptx, CAPE_EVALs_Playbook_v5, eval convo decks). Meetings explicitly flag consolidation as open action item. Archetype 18 (Parallel Creation Gap). SGI 90 CRITICAL. EvalCon deadline 2026-04-02 amplifies urgency (+5 per deadline rule). Automation target: canonical eval enablement template with role-appropriate audience variants.

patterns.label: Technical Artifact Distribution via Teams (Repos, ZIPs, Specs)
patterns.llmRationale: PM acts as manual distribution hub for working artifacts: GitHub repos, ZIP specs, markdown, YAML, Loop components, SharePoint links, demo templates. 8 occurrences/week. Archetype 8 (Cross-Tool Context Consolidation). Automation target: curated artifact registry that auto-surfaces relevant assets when team members query a project or topic, eliminating the PM-as-switchboard pattern.

patterns.label: Strategic Leadership Email Thread Participation (CAPE/Evals)
patterns.llmRationale: High-density leadership threads involving PM, Program Manager, Engineering Lead, Partner PM, and CAPE DL: Project Crucible Evals-First strategy, EvalCon announcements, customer playbook updates. Each thread requires reading long content, extracting actions, and synthesizing PM position. Archetype 20 (Expert Knowledge Broadcasting). Moderate automation: thread summarization and action extraction feasible; leadership positioning requires PM judgment.

patterns.label: Agent Architecture & MCP/Skills Scoping Discussions
patterns.llmRationale: Recurring Teams discussions and one URGENT email about MCP servers, CLI vs UI tradeoffs, WorkIQ integration, declarative agent grounding (170+ SharePoint sites), skill-creator/skill-detector scoping, Copilot Studio vs SDK decisions. Archetype 21 (Feasibility Escalation Thread) + Archetype 19 (Ambiguous-to-Artifact). Rising: new agentic initiatives pulling more architectural scoping. Structured feasibility templates could accelerate decisions.

patterns.label: Daily Personal Day-Prep Planning Page (Personal Ops Ritual)
patterns.llmRationale: PM creates a structured daily planning document each morning (e.g., '[Mar 19] Help me prepare for my day') on a repeating cadence rather than starting from scratch. Pattern is daily, sole-author, structurally consistent. NEW Archetype 27 (Personal Ops Ritual). Do NOT apply -15 solo penalty — this is Anti-Pattern 22 (Solo-Author Undervaluing). Daily frequency (5/week) gives outsized automation ROI: auto-populate from calendar, outstanding action items, email priorities, prior-day notes. GENERALIZABLE to any role.

patterns.label: EvalCon Content Planning & CAPE Positioning Discussions
patterns.llmRationale: Recurring Teams discussions about EvalCon (deadline 2026-04-02): what CAPE should present, how to frame blockers, submission process, leadership alignment. Archetype 3 (Status Report Assembly) + Deadline Demand Amplification active (+5 to eval patterns, deadline 13 days away). Rising: urgency increasing as deadline approaches. Content strategy requires PM judgment but briefing templates, deadline trackers, and submission checklists are automatable.

patterns.label: VOC & Cross-Org Navigation Routing Assistance
patterns.llmRationale: PM acts as informal routing layer between field engineers, MCS Analytics, and PG Eval teams. People ask PM to identify the right DRI for eval-related asks, VOC submission paths, and product feedback routing. Archetype 4 (Customer Ask Dedup/Routing). Deterministic routing logic: codifiable as a DRI routing table and org navigation skill that answers 'who owns X?' across the eval ecosystem without funneling through PM.

patterns.label: CAPE Camp AIR Immersive Training & Hackathon (Cascade Source)
patterns.llmRationale: Two full-day immersive events (Camp AIR) plus dozens of adjacent sessions totaling ~15 hours. CASCADE SOURCE: this single event amplified 6 downstream patterns. Apply 0.7x trend dampening to downstream patterns (meeting-notes, eval-deck, context-fragmentation, meeting-load, transcript-to-loop, artifact-distribution) to avoid falsely counting 6 independent surges. Archetype 22 (Quarterly Immersion Cadence). Next Camp AIR predicted June 2026. Pre-event prep automation (agendas, standups, debrief templates, hackathon artifact packaging) is high value.

candidateSkills.name: eval-design-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Answers inbound questions about evaluation strategy, metrics selection, eval depth and timing, and CLI tooling mechanics for AI agents. Operates as a codified substitute for the PM-as-sole-DRI bottleneck across three tiers: T1 auto-answer (scoping, framework FAQ, CLI syntax), T2 confirm (troubleshooting, grading), T3 route (production readiness, teaching).
triggerExamples: How many eval cases are enough for an agent going to production?
What metrics should I use for a retrieval-augmented agent — groundedness vs Compare Meaning?
My CSV import isn't working in the eval CLI — what format does it expect?
When in the agent development lifecycle should we run evals?
What is tool_call_accuracy and how do I interpret a score of 0.74?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Unblocks 15 Field Engineers and PMs per cycle who queue up the same PM for repeatable Q&A. BSI 90 EMERGENCY — T1 stubs must deploy within 2 cycles. With EvalCon 2026-04-02 approaching and 50+ external partner eval sessions per week (EDII 5.0+), the downstream multiplier is org-wide.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: After any meeting, generates a structured Loop-compatible notes document: Agenda recap, Key Decisions, Action Items with owners and due dates, Open Questions, and Next Steps. Works from meeting transcript, calendar metadata, or verbal summary.
triggerExamples: Create meeting notes for today's Evals sync
Extract action items from this transcript and assign owners
Summarize the decisions we made in this meeting
Set up a Loop page for the Project Crucible v-team weekly
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Chain head for the Meeting Output Cluster. Eliminates SGI 92 CRITICAL — the largest standardization gap in the dataset. Maximally portable: every PM, engineer, and manager attends meetings. Cross-source validated from both meeting workflow signals and Loop document signals.

candidateSkills.name: ado-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Triages Azure DevOps work-item email notifications by classifying each by type (Task, Bug, Requirement, Epic, Feature), determining whether PM action is required (assignee vs mentioned vs subscriber), and surfacing only the subset needing attention with a concise action summary.
triggerExamples: Triage my ADO email notifications from this week
Which of these Azure DevOps notifications actually need my attention?
Filter my ADO Evals notifications — I am only subscribed on most of these
Which bugs assigned to me are blocking others?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: 30 notifications per week with 90%+ automation ceiling. Highest-frequency email pattern in the dataset. Decomposition into 5 sub-classifiers (IcM, Coverage, Evals, Tasks, Epics) enables granular routing. Cumulative occurrences in prior data: 863 — overdue for decomposition.

candidateSkills.name: eval-enablement-standardizer
candidateSkills.description: Generates a canonical eval enablement deck or playbook from a standard template, customized by audience role (Field Engineer, Customer, Leadership). Eliminates the parallel-creation problem where each team member independently produces their own version of the same eval intro content.
triggerExamples: Create an eval intro deck for a new SI partner
Generate a CAPE evals playbook for the EvalCon submission
Make a customer-facing eval overview for a retail use case
We have five versions of the eval deck floating — consolidate them
candidateSkills.valueProposition: SGI 90 CRITICAL. Three team members independently created eval decks this cycle alone. EvalCon deadline 2026-04-02 and 50+ partner sessions per week make standardized materials urgent. Saves 90+ minutes per PM and Field Engineer per cycle.

candidateSkills.name: daily-briefing-composer
candidateSkills.description: Generates a structured daily planning page pre-populated from the user's calendar, outstanding action items, unread email priorities, and prior-day notes. Replaces the blank-page daily prep ritual with a one-click contextual briefing. Works for any role with M365 activity.
triggerExamples: Help me prepare for my day
What is on my agenda today and what should I prioritize?
Compile a morning briefing from my calendar and email
Create today's planning page — I have two external eval sessions and a leadership review
candidateSkills.valueProposition: New Archetype 27 (Personal Ops Ritual). 5 occurrences per cycle, 25 minutes per day = roughly 2 hours per week recovered. Sole-author patterns appear low-value but are highest-ROI per-person automations with maximal generalizability — every knowledge worker runs a morning planning ritual regardless of role, org, or industry.
